citation: Gilbert, B.R., Thornburg, Z.R., Brier, T.A., et al. Dynamics of chromosome organization in a minimal bacterial cell. Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology 11:1214962 (2023).
notes: Defines the chromosome polymer model reused in Thornburg 2026: Worm-Like Chain homopolymer at 10 bp per bead, giving ~54,300 beads for the 543 kbp Syn3A chromosome. Excluded-volume interactions between DNA strands and between DNA and boundary. SMC (condensin-like) loop extrusion modelled by anchoring one loop bead and progressively updating a 'hinge' to extrude hundreds-to-thousands of bp per loop. Topoisomerase action resolves topology via alternating loop updates + energy minimization + Brownian Dynamics steps. Simulated in LAMMPS + Kokkos GPU via the lab's btree_chromo_gpu tool. Not directly on our Layer-6 critical path - we don't model chromosome topology - but registering here for citation completeness and in case future simulator-biology work (Session 11+) wants a chromosome representation.
